contents.value:
```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
FROM python:3.12-slim
FROM --platform=linux/amd64 ubuntu:22.04
FROM scratch
```
**FROM** initializes a new build stage and sets the base image. Every Dockerfile must begin with a `FROM` instruction (only `ARG` may precede it). Multiple `FROM` instructions create multi-stage builds. Use `AS name` to reference the stage later with `COPY --from=name`.

```dockerfile
# Shell form
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl

# Exec form
RUN ["apt-get", "install", "-y", "curl"]

# BuildKit cache mount
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/var/cache/apt \
    apt-get update && apt-get install -y gcc

# Heredoc
RUN <<EOF
#!/bin/bash
set -e
apt-get update
apt-get install -y curl
EOF
```
**RUN** executes a command in a new layer. Combine related commands with `&&` and clean up in the same `RUN` to reduce layer size. Use `--mount=type=cache` for package manager caches.

```dockerfile
# Exec form (preferred)
CMD ["node", "server.js"]

# Shell form
CMD node server.js

# As ENTRYPOINT default params
ENTRYPOINT ["python"]
CMD ["app.py"]
```
**CMD** sets the default command for the container. Only the last `CMD` takes effect. Exec form is preferred because it does not invoke a shell. When used with `ENTRYPOINT`, `CMD` provides default arguments.

```dockerfile
# Exec form (preferred)
ENTRYPOINT ["python", "-m", "flask"]

# With CMD defaults
ENTRYPOINT ["python"]
CMD ["app.py"]

# With tini for signal handling
ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
```
**ENTRYPOINT** configures a container to run as an executable. Exec form is preferred. `CMD` arguments are appended. Override at runtime with `--entrypoint`. Use tini or dumb-init as PID 1 for proper signal handling.

```dockerfile
LABEL maintainer="dev@example.com"
LABEL version="1.0"

# OCI annotations
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.title="My App" \
      org.opencontainers.image.version="1.0.0" \
      org.opencontainers.image.authors="dev@example.com"
```
**LABEL** adds metadata to the image. Use OCI annotation keys (`org.opencontainers.image.*`) for interoperability. View labels with `docker inspect`.

```dockerfile
# Deprecated
MAINTAINER dev@example.com

# Use instead:
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.authors="dev@example.com"
```
**MAINTAINER** is deprecated. Use `LABEL org.opencontainers.image.authors` instead for setting the image author.

```dockerfile
EXPOSE 8080
EXPOSE 80 443
EXPOSE 53/udp
EXPOSE 8080/tcp 8443/tcp
```
**EXPOSE** documents which ports the container listens on. It does not actually publish ports — use `-p` or `-P` flags at runtime. Protocol defaults to TCP if not specified.

```dockerfile
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV APP_HOME=/app PATH="/app/bin:$PATH"

# Python best practice
ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
```
**ENV** sets environment variables that persist in the image and running containers. Values can be overridden with `docker run --env`. Use `ARG` for build-time-only variables to avoid leaking secrets.

```dockerfile
# Local file
ADD app.tar.gz /opt/app

# Remote URL
ADD https://example.com/file.txt /tmp/

# With checksum verification
ADD --checksum=sha256:abc123... https://example.com/bin /usr/local/bin/
```
**ADD** copies files into the image. Unlike COPY, ADD supports remote URLs and auto-extracts tar archives. Prefer `COPY` for simple file operations. Use `ADD` when you need tar extraction or remote downloads.

```dockerfile
COPY . /app
COPY --chown=1001:1001 . /app
COPY --chmod=755 entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
COPY --link package.json ./
```
**COPY** copies files from the build context into the image. Use `--from` to copy from another build stage (multi-stage). `--link` enables layer-independent copying for better cache reuse. Preferred over `ADD` for simple file copying.

```dockerfile
VOLUME /data
VOLUME ["/data", "/logs"]
```
**VOLUME** creates a mount point for externally mounted volumes. Data written to volumes persists independently of the container. Note: you cannot change volume contents during build after the VOLUME instruction.

```dockerfile
# By name
USER appuser

# By UID:GID
USER 1001:1001

# Best practice: create user first
RUN groupadd -r appuser && useradd -r -g appuser appuser
USER appuser
```
**USER** sets the user for subsequent `RUN`, `CMD`, and `ENTRYPOINT` instructions. Always run production containers as non-root for security. Create the user with `RUN` before switching.

```dockerfile
WORKDIR /app
WORKDIR src
WORKDIR /app/config
```
**WORKDIR** sets the working directory for `RUN`, `CMD`, `ENTRYPOINT`, `COPY`, and `ADD`. Creates the directory if it does not exist. Can be used multiple times; relative paths build on the previous `WORKDIR`. Prefer `WORKDIR` over `RUN cd`.

```dockerfile
# With default
ARG VERSION=latest
ARG NODE_ENV=production

# Before FROM (scoped to FROM)
ARG BASE_IMAGE=node:20-alpine
FROM $BASE_IMAGE

# Re-declare after FROM to use
ARG VERSION
RUN echo $VERSION
```
**ARG** defines a build-time variable. Pass values with `--build-arg`. ARGs defined before `FROM` are only available in `FROM`. After `FROM`, re-declare to use in subsequent instructions. ARGs do not persist in the running container (use `ENV` for that).

```dockerfile
ONBUILD COPY . /app
ONBUILD RUN npm install
```
**ONBUILD** registers a trigger instruction that fires when the image is used as a base. Useful for language-specific base images. The trigger runs before any instruction in the child Dockerfile.

```dockerfile
STOPSIGNAL SIGTERM
STOPSIGNAL SIGQUIT
STOPSIGNAL 9
```
**STOPSIGNAL** sets the signal sent to the container on `docker stop`. Default is SIGTERM. Use SIGQUIT for graceful shutdown with core dump, or SIGINT for applications expecting Ctrl+C.

```dockerfile
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1

# Using wget (Alpine)
HEALTHCHECK CMD wget --quiet --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8080/ || exit 1

# Disable inherited health check
HEALTHCHECK NONE
```
**HEALTHCHECK** defines how Docker tests if a container is working. The command exit code indicates health: 0 = healthy, 1 = unhealthy. Only the last `HEALTHCHECK` takes effect. Use `NONE` to disable checks inherited from a base image.

```dockerfile
# Linux: switch to bash
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]

# Windows: switch to PowerShell
SHELL ["powershell", "-Command"]
```
**SHELL** overrides the default shell for shell-form commands. Affects all subsequent `RUN`, `CMD`, and `ENTRYPOINT` instructions in shell form. Use to switch from sh to bash for better scripting features.

```dockerfile
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1.7
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:labs
```
**syntax** is a parser directive that sets the BuildKit frontend. Must be the very first line of the Dockerfile. Using `docker/dockerfile:1` ensures the latest stable Dockerfile syntax. The `labs` channel provides experimental features.

```dockerfile
# escape=\
# escape=`
```
**escape** is a parser directive that changes the escape character. Default is backslash (`\`). On Windows, use backtick (`` ` ``) since backslash is a path separator. Must appear before any instruction or comment.

```dockerfile
# Cache mount (package manager cache)
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip \
    pip install -r requirements.txt

# Bind mount (read files without copying)
RUN --mount=type=bind,source=config.json,target=/tmp/config.json \
    cat /tmp/config.json

# Secret mount (never persisted)
RUN --mount=type=secret,id=npmrc,target=/root/.npmrc \
    npm install

# SSH mount
RUN --mount=type=ssh git clone git@github.com:org/repo.git
```
**--mount** (BuildKit) attaches additional filesystems during `RUN`. Types: cache (persistent cache), bind (read context files), secret (sensitive data), ssh (agent forwarding), tmpfs (temp storage). Mounts are not committed to the image layer.

```dockerfile
COPY --link package.json ./
COPY --link --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
```
**--link** flag for `COPY`/`ADD` creates a layer independent of previous layers. When the base image changes, linked layers can be reused without rebuilding. Significantly improves cache hit rates in multi-stage builds.

```dockerfile
ADD --checksum=sha256:24454f830cdb571e2c4ad15481119c43b3cafd48dd869a9b2015d0c... \
    https://example.com/binary /usr/local/bin/
```
**--checksum** verifies the SHA256 hash of a remote file added with `ADD`. Ensures the downloaded file has not been tampered with. Only supported with URL sources.

```dockerfile
COPY --chmod=755 entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
COPY --chmod=644 config.yaml /etc/app/
ADD --chmod=755 https://example.com/binary /usr/local/bin/
```
**--chmod** sets file permissions on `COPY`/`ADD` operations. Uses octal notation. Avoids needing a separate `RUN chmod` layer, which saves space and build time.

```dockerfile
COPY --chown=node:node package.json ./
COPY --chown=1001:1001 . /app
ADD --chown=www-data:www-data files/ /var/www/
```
**--chown** sets ownership on `COPY`/`ADD` operations. Accepts user/group names or UID/GID numbers. Avoids a separate `RUN chown` layer. Names are resolved from the image `/etc/passwd` and `/etc/group`.

```dockerfile
FROM --platform=linux/amd64 node:20-alpine
FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:1.22 AS builder
FROM --platform=$TARGETPLATFORM debian:bookworm-slim
```
**--platform** in `FROM` sets the target platform for that build stage. Use `$BUILDPLATFORM` for stages that run native tools (compilers). Use `$TARGETPLATFORM` or explicit values for the final image. Essential for cross-compilation workflows.

```dockerfile
RUN --network=none pip install --no-deps -r requirements.txt
RUN --network=host curl http://host-service:8080/
```
**--network** controls network access during `RUN`. `none` disables all networking (security hardening). `host` uses the host network stack. Default provides normal container networking.

```dockerfile
RUN --security=insecure apt-get install -y privileged-package
```
**--security=insecure** runs the command with elevated privileges. Requires the `security.insecure` entitlement to be enabled. Use sparingly and only when absolutely necessary.

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
RUN npm ci

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
RUN npm run build

FROM nginx:alpine
COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
```
**Build stages** are created by each `FROM` instruction. Name them with `AS` and reference with `COPY --from`. Each stage starts fresh. Only the last stage (or `--target` stage) produces the final image.

```dockerfile
# Build stage: has all tools
FROM golang:1.22 AS builder
COPY . .
RUN go build -o /app

# Final stage: minimal
FROM gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12
COPY --from=builder /app /app
ENTRYPOINT ["/app"]
```
**Multi-stage builds** use multiple `FROM` statements. Build stages contain compilers and tools; the final stage contains only the application. This can reduce image size by 90%+. Use `--target` to build a specific stage.

```dockerfile
# Good: dependencies first (cached), source second
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .

# Bad: any source change invalidates npm ci cache
COPY . .
RUN npm ci
```
**Build cache** reuses layers when instructions and their inputs are unchanged. Order instructions from least to most frequently changing. Mount caches for package managers with `--mount=type=cache`. Use `.dockerignore` to exclude files that cause unnecessary cache invalidation.

```dockerfile
# .dockerignore
node_modules
.git
*.md
Dockerfile
```
**Build context** is the set of files sent to the Docker daemon. Defined by the path in `docker build <path>`. Use `.dockerignore` to exclude large or sensitive files. A smaller context means faster builds. The build context is the root for `COPY` and `ADD` source paths.

```dockerfile
# Each instruction creates a layer:
FROM ubuntu:22.04          # Layer 1: base
RUN apt-get update         # Layer 2: package index
COPY . /app                # Layer 3: app files
RUN npm install            # Layer 4: dependencies

# Combine to reduce layers:
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
```
**Layers** are filesystem diffs stacked to form an image. Each `RUN`, `COPY`, and `ADD` creates a layer. Layers are cached and shared. Minimize layer count by combining related commands. Clean up temporary files in the same layer to reduce size.

```dockerfile
FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:1.22 AS builder
ARG TARGETPLATFORM
ARG TARGETOS
ARG TARGETARCH
RUN GOOS=$TARGETOS GOARCH=$TARGETARCH go build -o /app
```
**BUILDPLATFORM** is an automatic build arg containing the platform of the node performing the build (e.g., `linux/amd64`). Use it with `FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM` to run build tools natively, then cross-compile for the target platform.

```dockerfile
FROM --platform=$TARGETPLATFORM debian:bookworm-slim
ARG TARGETPLATFORM
RUN echo "Building for $TARGETPLATFORM"
```
**TARGETPLATFORM** is the target platform specified by `--platform` (e.g., `linux/arm64`). Automatically set by BuildKit. Use it to download platform-specific binaries or configure builds.

```dockerfile
ARG TARGETOS
ARG TARGETARCH
RUN GOOS=$TARGETOS GOARCH=$TARGETARCH go build -o /app
```
**TARGETOS** contains the OS component of the target platform (e.g., `linux`, `windows`). Used for cross-compilation, especially with Go.

```dockerfile
ARG TARGETARCH
RUN curl -L https://example.com/binary-${TARGETARCH} -o /usr/local/bin/binary
```
**TARGETARCH** contains the architecture component (e.g., `amd64`, `arm64`, `arm`). Use to download or build platform-specific binaries.

```dockerfile
ARG TARGETARCH
ARG TARGETVARIANT
RUN echo "arch=$TARGETARCH variant=$TARGETVARIANT"
```
**TARGETVARIANT** contains the variant component (e.g., `v7` for `arm/v7`). Useful for ARM variant-specific builds.

hovers.--mount=type=bind:
## --mount=type=bind

```dockerfile
RUN --mount=type=bind,source=<src>,target=<dest>[,from=<stage>] <command>
```

Bind mount a directory from the build context or another stage.

```dockerfile
# Mount source code read-only
RUN --mount=type=bind,target=/src go build -o /app /src/cmd/server

# Mount from another stage
FROM build AS deps
RUN --mount=type=bind,from=deps,source=/go/pkg,target=/go/pkg go build
```

**Key options:**
- `source` — path in context/stage (default: root)
- `target` — mount point in container
- `from` — source stage name
- `rw` — read-write (default: read-only)
hovers.--mount=type=cache:
## --mount=type=cache

```dockerfile
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=<path>[,id=<id>][,sharing=shared] <command>
```

Persistent cache directory across builds. Dramatically speeds up package installs.

```dockerfile
# Go module cache
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod \
    go build -o /app .

# npm cache
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm \
    npm ci

# pip cache
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip \
    pip install -r requirements.txt

# apt cache
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/var/cache/apt \
    --mount=type=cache,target=/var/lib/apt \
    apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl
```

**Options:**
- `id` — unique cache ID (default: target path)
- `sharing` — `shared` (default), `private`, `locked`
hovers.--mount=type=secret:
## --mount=type=secret

```dockerfile
RUN --mount=type=secret,id=<id>[,target=<path>] <command>
```

Mount a secret file without baking it into the image layer.

```dockerfile
# Access secret during build
RUN --mount=type=secret,id=npmrc,target=/root/.npmrc \
    npm ci

RUN --mount=type=secret,id=aws,target=/root/.aws/credentials \
    aws s3 cp s3://bucket/file .
```

**Pass secret at build time:**
```bash
docker build --secret id=npmrc,src=$HOME/.npmrc .
docker build --secret id=aws,src=$HOME/.aws/credentials .
```

**Note:** Secret is never saved in image layers.
hovers.--mount=type=ssh:
## --mount=type=ssh

```dockerfile
RUN --mount=type=ssh <command>
```

Forward SSH agent to access private repos during build.

```dockerfile
RUN --mount=type=ssh \
    git clone git@github.com:private/repo.git /app

RUN --mount=type=ssh \
    pip install git+ssh://git@github.com/private/package.git
```

**Build with SSH:**
```bash
docker build --ssh default .
# or specific key:
docker build --ssh default=$HOME/.ssh/id_rsa .
```

**Prerequisite:** `ssh-add` your key before building.
hovers.--mount=type=tmpfs:
## --mount=type=tmpfs

```dockerfile
RUN --mount=type=tmpfs,target=<path>[,size=<bytes>] <command>
```

Mount a tmpfs (RAM-backed) filesystem.

```dockerfile
RUN --mount=type=tmpfs,target=/tmp \
    compile-something --temp-dir=/tmp
```

Useful for build steps that need fast temporary storage.
hovers.BUILDARCH:
## BUILDARCH

```dockerfile
ARG BUILDARCH
```

Automatic build argument containing the CPU architecture of the build machine.

**Values:** `amd64`, `arm64`, `arm`, `386`, `ppc64le`, `s390x`

```dockerfile
FROM golang:1.22
ARG BUILDARCH
RUN echo "Building on: $BUILDARCH"
```

**Related:** `BUILDOS`, `BUILDPLATFORM`, `BUILDVARIANT`
hovers.BUILDOS:
## BUILDOS

```dockerfile
ARG BUILDOS
```

Automatic build argument containing the OS of the build machine.

**Values:** `linux`, `windows`, `darwin`

```dockerfile
ARG BUILDOS
RUN echo "Build OS: $BUILDOS"
```
hovers.BUILDVARIANT:
## BUILDVARIANT

```dockerfile
ARG BUILDVARIANT
```

Automatic build argument for the CPU variant (e.g., `v7` for ARMv7).

```dockerfile
ARG BUILDVARIANT
RUN echo "Variant: ${BUILDVARIANT:-none}"
```
hovers..dockerignore:
## .dockerignore

File that controls what gets sent to the Docker daemon as build context.

```
# .dockerignore
.git
.gitignore
node_modules
npm-debug.log
Dockerfile*
docker-compose*
.dockerignore
.env
*.md
.vscode
__pycache__
*.pyc
dist
build
.tox
coverage
.nyc_output
```

**Pattern syntax:**
- `*` matches any sequence (excluding path separators)
- `**` matches any number of directories
- `!` excludes from ignore (re-include)
- `#` comment line

**Example with exception:**
```
*.md
!README.md
```

**Why it matters:** Large build contexts slow down builds. Always exclude `node_modules`, `.git`, etc.
hovers.heredoc:
## Heredoc Syntax

Inline multi-line content in Dockerfile (BuildKit 1.4+).

**Inline files:**
```dockerfile
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
COPY <<EOF /app/config.json
{"port": 3000}
EOF
```

**Multi-line RUN:**
```dockerfile
RUN <<EOF
apt-get update
apt-get install -y curl git
rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
EOF
```

**With interpreter:**
```dockerfile
RUN <<EOF python3
import os
print(os.environ)
EOF
```

**Multiple files:**
```dockerfile
COPY <<app.py <<requirements.txt /app/
from flask import Flask
app = Flask(__name__)
app.py
flask>=3.0
requirements.txt
```

**Requires:** `# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1` or BuildKit >= 0.10
hovers.--parents:
## --parents (COPY flag)

```dockerfile
COPY --parents <src> <dest>
```

Preserve the parent directory structure when copying.

```dockerfile
# Without --parents:
COPY src/app/config.json /dest/
# Result: /dest/config.json

# With --parents:
COPY --parents src/app/config.json /dest/
# Result: /dest/src/app/config.json
```

**Requires:** BuildKit
hovers.--keep-git-dir:
## --keep-git-dir

```dockerfile
ADD --keep-git-dir=true <git-url> <dest>
```

Keep the .git directory when adding from a Git repository URL.

```dockerfile
ADD --keep-git-dir=true https://github.com/user/repo.git#main /src
```

By default, .git is stripped. Useful when build needs Git metadata (version, commit hash).
